item.title: China poised for Q1 GDP growth rebound but Iran war dims 2026 outlook: Reuters poll
item.description: China's economy likely regained some momentum in the first quarter on solid вҖҢexports, but growth is expected to cool over the rest of 2026 as the Middle East crisis threatens to choke corporate profits and sap overseas demand, a Reuters poll showed.

item.title: World Bank chief sounds alarm about looming jobs crisis even after war ends
item.description: The Middle East war will dominate global finance officials' talks this week in Washington, but World Bank President Ajay Banga is sounding вҖӢthe alarm about a bigger, looming crisis: a huge gap in jobs for the 1.2 billion people who will reach working age in developing вҖҢcountries in the next 10 to 15 years.

item.title: Chinese AI startup StepFun to unwind offshore structure to pave way for IPO, sources say
item.description: Chinese AI agent StepFun is unwinding its offshore incorporation structure to pave the way for a planned Hong Kong initial public offering, three sources said, вҖӢas Beijing tightens scrutiny of a system widely used for overseas fundraising.

item.title: Basic-Fit data breach exposes details of gym members in Netherlands, elsewhere
item.description: Gym operator Basic-Fit has suffered a data breach involving "active members вҖӢin several countries" and вҖӢaffecting around 200,000 of its clients вҒ in the Netherlands alone, вҖӢit said on Monday.

item.title: UK fintech firm Wise's quarterly cross-border volumes surge ahead of Nasdaq debut
item.description: British fintech group Wise said вҖҢon Monday cross-border transaction volumes rose 26% in the fourth quarter to 49.4 billion вҖӢpounds ($66.2 billion), reinforcing its expectation that вҖӢannual profit margins will land near вҒ the top end of its forecast вҖӢrange.

item.title: Baker Hughes sells Waygate unit to Hexagon for about $1.45 billion
item.description: Oilfield services provider Baker Hughes sold its Waygate Technologies unit вҖӢto Swedish industrial technology group Hexagon вҖҢfor about $1.45 billion in cash, the two companies said on Monday.

item.title: Exclusive: Luxury brands face profits squeeze as Iran conflict shrinks Dubai Mall sales
item.description: Sales at Europe's biggest luxury brands have shrunk in Dubai and Abu Dhabi as the Iran conflict hit the sector's fastest-growing market in the latest setback for the $400 billion industry вҖӢwhose value has contracted over the last three years.

item.title: China's exports set to lose momentum as Iran war undercuts AI-driven boom: Reuters poll
item.description: China's export engine likely slowed in March вҖӢas buyers chasing an AI-fuelled future confront the hard reality of war in the Middle East, which вҖҢhas sparked an energy shock and revived market anxiety of past Gulf conflicts.

item.title: Morning Bid: Oil surges on US blockade of Iran
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Tom Westbrook

item.title: TSMC likely to book fourth straight quarter of record profit onВ insatiable AI demand
item.description: TSMC, the world's largest manufacturer of advanced artificial intelligence chips, will likely notch вҖҢup a fourth consecutive quarter of record earnings with a 50% surge in net profit for January-March thanks to booming demand for AI infrastructure.

item.title: Bank of Korea governor nominee vows to respond to excessive won weakness
item.description: The Bank of Korea's governor nominee said it would be necessary to вҖӢrespond properly if the won fell excessively, in written remarks he вҖҢsubmitted on Monday to parliament ahead of a parliamentary confirmation hearing on Wednesday.

item.title: McDonald's to add energy drinks, crafted sodas to US menus, WSJ reports
item.description: McDonald's is preparing to introduce a Red вҖӢBull Dragonberry Energizer as part of вҖҢa broader revamp of its cold drink menu at its U.S. restaurants later this year, the Wall Street вҖӢJournal reported on Sunday, citing company вҖӢdocuments.

item.title: GFL nears deal to buy Secure Waste for over $4.3 billion, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Waste management company GFL Environmental is nearing a deal to вҖӢacquire Canada's Secure Waste Infrastructure вҖҢin a deal valued at over C$6 billion ($4.33 billion), Bloomberg News reported on вҖӢSunday, citing a person familiar вҖӢwith the matter.

item.title: Meatpacker JBS reaches tentative agreement with striking Colorado workers
item.description: Workers at JBS ratified a tentative two-year agreement with the world's largest meat company, вҖӢcovering nearly 3,800 workers at the JBS flagship beef вҖҢprocessing plant in Greeley, Colorado, according to statements from both the union and the company on Sunday.

item.title: Dollar strengthens as peace talks falter, US blockade of Iran's ports to begin
item.description: The dollar strengthened to the highest level in a week in вҖӢa broad rally against most of its вҖҢpeers in early Asian trade on Monday as peace talks between the U.S. and Iran broke down and the U.S. вҖӢNavy prepared a blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: China's Victory Giant seeks $2.2 billion in Hong Kong listing amid market turmoil
item.description: China's Victory Giant launched a Hong Kong share sale on Monday seeking to raise up вҖҢto HK$17.49 billion ($2.23 billion), pressing ahead with what could rank among the city's largest listings this year despite global market turmoil driven by Middle East tensions.

item.title: Dollar jumps as failed US-Iran peace talks spark safe-haven push
item.description: US stock futures fell more than 1% in late Sunday U.S. trading.

item.title: Oil bounces back above $100 after US, Iran talks end in stalemate
item.description: Oil prices jumped above $100 вҖӢa barrel on вҖҢMonday as the U.S. Navy prepared вҖӢto blockade the вҖӢStrait of Hormuz after вҒ talks between вҖӢthe U.S. and Iran вҖӢfailed to reach a deal to end вҖӢthe war.

item.title: US FTC in settlement talks with ad companies in boycott probe, WSJ reports
item.description: The U.S. Federal Trade вҖӢCommission is вҖҢnegotiating a potential settlement with вҖӢseveral major вҖӢadvertising companies to resolve вҒ a probe вҖӢinto whether вҖӢthey violated federal antitrust laws by вҖӢcoordinating boycotts вҖӢagainst platforms, including Elon вҖҢMuskвҖҷs вҒ X, the Wall Street Journal reported on вҖӢSunday, вҖӢciting вҒ people familiar with the вҖӢmatter.

item.title: Dollar jumps as failed US/Iran peace talks spark fresh safe-haven push
item.description: The dollar jumped against other major currencies in thin trading late on Sunday, as investors sought the relative safety of the U.S. currency after marathon talks between вҖӢWashington and Tehran failed to yield a peace deal, plunging markets into a seventh вҖҢweek of uncertainty.

item.title: GSK sees blockbuster potential in targeted cancer therapy after promising early data
item.description: British drugmaker GSK's experimental targeted cancer drug Mo-rez has blockbuster potential, its head of oncology research has told reporters, after early data showed the drug helped вҖӢshrink tumors in patients with advanced, hard-to-treat cancers.

item.title: UK financial regulators rush to assess risks of AnthropicвҖҷs latest AI model, FT reports
item.description: UK financial regulators вҖӢare holding вҖҢurgent talks with вҖӢthe вҖӢgovernment's cyber security agency вҒ and вҖӢmajor banks вҖӢto assess risks posed by вҖӢthe вҖӢnew artificial intelligence вҖҢmodel вҒ from Anthropic, the Financial Times вҖӢreported вҖӢon вҒ Sunday.

item.title: China's Chery looking to expand car production in Europe, top executives say
item.description: Chery is looking to expand car production in Europe through partnerships with other automakers allowing it to use existing factories, top executives at the Chinese carmaker said вҖӢat an event in Paris.

item.title: Economic shock of Middle East war to cast shadow over IMF, World Bank meetings
item.description: Top finance officials from around the world will convene in Washington this week under the shadow of the war in the Middle East, which has delivered a third major shock to the global economy after the COVID вҖӢpandemic and Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

item.title: Iran aims to restore majority of refining capability within two months, oil ministry official says
item.description: Iran expects to restore most вҖӢdamaged refining and вҖҢdistribution facilities to 70вҖ“80% of their pre-attack вҖӢcapacity within one вҖӢto two months, a вҒ senior oil official вҖӢsaid, as authorities вҖӢwork to recover from a wave of strikes on вҖӢenergy infrastructure.

item.title: Saudi Arabia restores full capacity on East-West oil pipeline to 7 million bpd after attacks
item.description: Saudi Arabia has restored full oil pumping capacity through the East-West вҖӢpipeline to about seven million barrels per day, it вҖҢsaid on Sunday, days after providing an assessment of damage on its energy sector from attacks during the Iran conflict.

item.title: South Korea close to securing oil supplies from Kazakhstan, minister says
item.description: South Korea is close to securing crude oil supplies from Kazakhstan, the industry вҖӢminister said on Sunday, as the вҖҢcountry looks for alternative energy sources amid the war in the Middle East.

item.title: BOJ policy to boost yen could be an option to curb inflation, Japanese minister says
item.description: Bank of Japan monetary policy could be an option to tame rising prices by boosting the yen, вҖӢJapan's trade minister said on Sunday, as the central вҖҢbank weighs raising interest rates this month to counter inflation spurred by the Iran war.

item.title: Israeli firm Black Cube confirms Cyprus sting to 'expose corruption'
item.description: A private spy firm founded by former members of Israeli intelligence has confirmed its involvement in a sting operation targeting state officials вҖӢand private interests in Cyprus, saying it wanted to expose вҖҢcorruption.

item.title: Chinese investment in Brazil pivots from power dams to ice cream, courting consumers
item.description: For Chinese ice cream and beverage chain Mixue, which already has more stores than Starbucks or McDonald's, a cheerful snowman mascot on Sao Paulo's most famous avenue signals a new phase of its global expansion.
